author: @richardseelye9938
text: Very nice. I have to go back to that cleaning solution article.  I will look as see if you made the sonic cleaner as well.

author: @richardsandwell2285
text:
What a challenge but you saw it through and it looks great. I am not sure what it is about bikes, I have found perfect ones with lightweight Aluminium Frames just dumped in a skip, or simply abandoned, yet leave any bike outside a shop without an hardened D-Shackle lock and it will get stolen in seconds. 
It seems they have no value to the owners her purchased them, much like vacuum cleaners and microwaves I suppose.

author: @jimmycorkhill1390
text: I had missed this video, excellent. Best wishes.

author: @____________________________.x
text: Those shocks will have oil inside, and so will all the wheel and lower bracket bearings. You’ve done about 1/10 of the work there, but kudos for recycling a bike

author: @BlueJeebs

author: @jonnyswalk_7
text:
Great Video Robert 👍 You mentioned a “miracle cleaning solution” - would it be good for cleaning really ground on brake dust etc from old car wheel trims or could you recommend some other home made miracle cleaning solution. I was made redundant several years ago,have been unwell since,but I try to collect old wheel trims,clean them up and then sell them on EBay for some pocket money. I realise this isn’t necessarily related,so no worries if you’ve not got the answer or time to reply 👍 
Thanks again - always inspiring 👍 
Jonathan from South Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿
